title: Hull Bridge (1) Halifax Memorial
location_name: Hull Bridge (1), East Riding of Yorkshire
crew_summary: 6 killed including pilot Flg Off. Charles James Keirl

external_narratives.summary: Halifax JB874 (coded EY-E) of 78 Squadron collided with Halifax BB373 (EY-K) at 04:30 on 24 August 1943 over Hull Bridge, Tickton, 2 miles NE of Beverley. Both crews had bombed Berlin and were being diverted -- first to Leconfield, then to Hutton Cranswick. Six of the seven crew of JB874 were killed; one survived. This was a disastrous night for 78 Squadron, which lost five aircraft and thirty-one airmen.
key_details: Diverted from Leconfield to Hutton Cranswick
78 Squadron lost five aircraft and 31 airmen that night

external_narratives.summary: IWM registers the memorial to the aircrew of 78 Squadron killed in the collision over Tickton on 24 August 1943. The memorial commemorates both crews from the two Halifax bombers that collided.

CWGC features a story about Pilot Officer Edward Platt, navigator aboard one of the 78 Squadron aircraft lost that night, providing verified service records and burial details for the casualties of this tragic collision.
key_details: CWGC verified service records for crew

external_narratives.summary: War Memorials Online records the 78 Squadron memorial, noting its location near the Crown and Anchor pub at Hull Bridge, close to the crash site.
key_details: Memorial near Crown and Anchor pub, Hull Bridge

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
